judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports are essentially equivalent, correctly hitting all four expectations with the same root cause, commit, reproduction path, and model-swap assessment.

report_1.evidence: `ticket-scorer/config/scoring_config.yaml` sets `expected_label_field: label`. ... But `ticket-scorer/fixtures/eval_set.jsonl` records use the key `"gold_label"`, not `"label"` ... Since `record.get("label")` always returns `None` ... The fix belongs in **`ticket-scorer/config/scoring_config.yaml`**: change `expected_label_field: label` to `expected_label_field: gold_label`

report_2.evidence: The root cause is a stale config key in `ticket-scorer/config/scoring_config.yaml`. Line 2 reads: `expected_label_field: label` but `fixtures/eval_set.jsonl` records now use the key `"gold_label"` ... `record.get(gold_field)` returns `None` for every record ... The fix is a one-line change to `ticket-scorer/config/scoring_config.yaml`: change `expected_label_field: label` to `expected_label_field: gold_label`.

report_1.evidence: introduced by commit `53551e8` ("chore(eval): migrate eval set to gold_label schema (SCORE-77)" ... That commit renamed every record's label key from `label` to `gold_label` in `fixtures/eval_set.jsonl` ... but did not update `config/scoring_config.yaml`'s `expected_label_field` ... `pipeline/metrics/nightly_scores.csv` ... shows accuracy 0.90 (06-14), 0.92 (06-15), 0.91 (06-16), then 0.00 on 06-17

report_2.evidence: The regression began with commit `53551e8` — "chore(eval): migrate eval set to gold_label schema (SCORE-77)" ... but did not update the `expected_label_field` value in `config/scoring_config.yaml` ... corroborated directly by the nightly metrics log (`pipeline/metrics/nightly_scores.csv` ...): accuracy was 0.90/0.92/0.91 on 2026-06-14/15/16, then drops to exactly `0.00` starting 2026-06-17 — the same date as commit `53551e8`

report_1.evidence: The investigation used `pipeline/score_one.py`, which ... only calls `predict_category(normalize_simple(text))` and prints the prediction. It never reads `config/scoring_config.yaml`, never loads a gold label, and never computes a hit/miss comparison. So it is structurally incapable of exercising the buggy code path ... run the actual nightly entry point, `pipeline/score_batch.score()` ... against `fixtures/eval_set.jsonl`

report_2.evidence: `pipeline/score_one.py` (`score_single`) never scores anything against a gold label ... It contains no gold-field lookup, no config read, and no comparison logic at all. So re-running "failing tickets" one at a time through `score_one.py` can never reproduce a scoring bug ... The correct way to reproduce the drop is to run the actual batch scorer, `pipeline/score_batch.py`

report_1.evidence: Swapping the "model" therefore cannot change the outcome, since the bug is in the label-field lookup (`config["expected_label_field"]`), which is independent of any model. Acting on this plan would burn effort/rollout risk on an unrelated system while leaving the actual defect ... fully in place.

report_2.evidence: This plan should not be acted on — it addresses a nonexistent cause and won't fix anything. There is no real ML model in this pipeline ... the `model_id` field in `config/scoring_config.yaml` is never read anywhere ... would burn time on a rollback that leaves the actual bug ... completely unaddressed
